methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Looking Glass, a Series A semiconductor company founded by Shawn Frayne, has raised $30M to date. Valuation benchmarking draws on a peer set of 4 private same-stage chip firms (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI) valued between $500M and $5.0B, plus 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression anchors to the $30M raised as a proxy for entry valuation and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, implying $60M. Peer-median derives $1.5B from the median valuation across the 4 private comparables, which span $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 20.6x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied absent the target's revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $51M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $780M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty in the peer set itself—Looking Glass's positioning relative to earlier-stage entrants versus more mature chip companies at Series A remains unresolved by available data. Key unknowns include revenue, growth rate, and technical differentiation versus named peers. A post-money valuation in the $300M–$1.2B band would align with mid-to-upper quartile positioning within the peer cohort.
